estop: verb. To impede or bar by estoppel.
its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.
opes: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ope
opt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Opata.
opts: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of opt
pes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranian Persian.
peso: noun. A former unit of currency in Spain and Spain's colonies, worth 8 reales; the Spanish dollar.
pest: noun. A pestilence, i.e. a deadly epidemic, a deadly plague.
pesto: noun. A sauce, especially for pasta, originating from the Genoa region in Italy, made from basil, garlic, pine nuts, olive oil and cheese
pet: noun. An animal kept as a companion or otherwise for pleasure, rather than for some practical benefit or use.
pis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pijin.
piso: noun. Alternative form of peso (“currency of Philippines”).
pit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pitta-Pitta.
pivot: noun. A thing on which something turns; specifically a metal pointed pin or short shaft in machinery, such as the end of an axle or spindle.
pose: noun. Common cold, head cold; catarrh.
posit: noun. Something that is posited; a postulate.
positive: adjective. Included, present, characterized by affirmation.
post: noun. A long dowel or plank protruding from the ground; a fencepost; a lightpost.
pot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Potawatomi.
potsie: noun. Any of the marbles located within a shallow hole (or "pot") used in certain variants of the game.
psi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeast Pashayi.
sei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seri.
sept: noun. A clan, tribe, or family, proceeding from a common progenitor; especially, one of the ancient clans of Ireland.
sip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sikkimese.
sipe: noun. Slit in a tire to drain away surface water and improve traction.
sit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sino-Tibetan languages.
site: noun. The place where anything is fixed; situation; local position
sop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Songe.
sopite: verb. To put to sleep, or to quieten.
sot: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Sotho.
spit: noun. A thin metal or wooden rod on which meat is skewered for cooking, often over a fire.
spite: noun. Ill will or hatred toward another, accompanied with the desire to unjustifiably irritate, annoy, or thwart; a want to disturb or put
spiv: noun. A smartly dressed person who trades in illicit, black-market or stolen goods, especially during World War II.
spot: noun. A round or irregular patch on the surface of a thing having a different color, texture etc. and generally round in shape.
step: noun. An advance or movement made from one foot to the other; a pace.
stipe: noun. The stem of a mushroom, kelp, etc.
stop: noun. ITU & IMO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for full stop / period.
stope: noun. A mining excavation in the form of a terrace of steps.
stove: noun. A heater, a closed apparatus to burn fuel for the warming of a room.
tip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Trimuris.
tis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masadiit Itneg.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
top: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Papantla Totonac.
tope: verb. To drink excessively; to get drunk.
topi: noun. An antelope of the species Damaliscus korrigum syn.
tops: adverb. At the very most; as a maximum.
vest: noun. A sleeveless garment that buttons down the front, worn over a shirt, and often as part of a suit; a waistcoat.
vet: noun. A veterinarian or veterinary surgeon.
veto: noun. A political right to disapprove of (and thereby stop) the process of a decision, a law etc.
vie: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Vietnamese.
vies: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of vie
vis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Vishavan.
vise: noun. An instrument consisting of two jaws, closing by a screw, lever, cam, or the like, for holding work, as in filing.
visit: verb. To habitually go to (someone in distress, sickness etc.) to comfort them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vote: noun. A formalized choice on legally relevant measures such as employment or appointment to office or a proceeding about a legal dispute.
